kpis.label: Rev / event (indexed)
kpis.sub: vs 1.0 in 2022 · illustrative, not a reported metric

series.label: US legal sports-betting handle
series.source: American Gaming Association Commercial Gaming Revenue Tracker
series.note: 2022 ≈ $93.8B (derived from 2023 handle ÷ 1.278) — pull exact 2021–2022 from AGA CY2021/CY2022 reports. Q1 2026 handle $43.52B (partial year, don't plot on annual axis).

series.label: US sports-betting GGR & hold %
series.source: American Gaming Association Commercial Gaming Revenue Tracker

series.label: US in-play as % of GGR
series.source: Modeled — operator decks + analyst notes (not a reported series)
series.note: No reported series exists — modeled from operator decks (Flutter/FanDuel, DraftKings) + analyst notes. Pick % of GGR vs % of handle and stay consistent.

series.label: US adults who bet (penetration)
series.note: ~20% of US adults placed a legal bet in 2025, up from 12% in 2023; ~50% of population still can't legally bet. Estimate, not a hard count. (Replaces seeded 2024:12/2026:20 with correct years.)

series.label: US states with legal sports betting
series.note: ~31 live as of 2026 — backfill the time series

series.label: Prediction-market volume (Kalshi / Polymarket)
series.note: The 'expanding pie' line — new venues that still settle on official data

series.label: GENI group revenue
series.source: GENI FY press releases / 6-Ks, SEC CIK 1834489
series.note: FY group revenue, reported actuals. 2026 guidance tracked separately (see revenue guidance series).

series.label: GENI revenue — guidance
series.note: Anchored at 2025 actual so the dashed guide line connects to the solid reported line. PF includes full-year Legend.

series.label: Betting Tech segment revenue
series.note: Q1 2026 = $146.2M (+33%). Backfill 2021–2023 from filings.

series.label: Media Tech segment revenue
series.source: GENI FY press releases (2025 Media derived: 669.5 − 471.5 − 53.5)
series.note: 2025 figure derived from group less Betting less Sports Tech. Q1 2026 = $41.7M. Confirm exact 2025 Media from 20-F.

series.label: Sports Tech & Services revenue
series.note: ⚠️ SEGMENT BREAK: discontinued as a separate reported segment from Q1 2026 — GENI now reports two segments (Betting + Media). Do not extend past 2025.

series.label: Group net loss (magnitude)
series.note: Reported as positive magnitudes. Q1 2026 net loss $55.5M. 2025 loss widened on NFL warrant SBC + litigation — context for the 'adjusted vs GAAP' bear point.

series.label: GENI betting revenue ÷ US GGR (take of market)
series.note: GENI's 'take' of the market over time. Derive once both inputs are loaded.

series.label: GENI vs Sportradar — official data rights split
series.note: Duopoly controls >90% of official league data rights

series.label: Leagues / events under exclusive rights
series.note: NFL through 2030 Super Bowl; Serie A through 2029; FIBA 10-yr through 2035. Backfill count by year from investor decks.

series.label: GENI revenue growth vs US handle growth
series.note: Out-growing the market = share gain via mix / cross-sell. Derive once both inputs are loaded.

series.label: Adj EBITDA margin
series.note: 2021 margin + 2026/28 guide tracked separately.

series.label: Adj EBITDA margin — guidance
series.note: Anchored at 2025 actual (20.4%) so the dashed guide connects to the solid reported margin line.

series.label: Group adj EBITDA
series.note: 2021 unconfirmed (near breakeven) — leave out until 20-F check.

series.label: Monetization per event (indexed, 2022 ≈ 1.0)
series.source: Internal estimate — not a reported GENI metric
series.note: Directional index of betting revenue ÷ events covered as in-play mix, BetVision attach & ads layer on

series.label: Group revenue ÷ rights cost (spread over fixed base)
series.note: Q1 2026: rev $187.9M ÷ data/streaming rights cost $85.6M = 2.20. Backfill annual once full-year rights cost pulled from each 20-F. Higher = better monetization of the fixed rights base.

series.label: In-play vs pre-match take-rate
series.note: Company range ~2.5–3× — not a time series.

series.label: BetVision in-play attach rate & avg bet size
series.note: 76% in-play attach in inaugural NFL season; bets ~2.5× larger. Point disclosures, not yet a series.

series.label: Media / ad revenue per user (FanHub + Legend)
series.note: Legend network: 320M visits / 118M users

series.label: Incremental adj EBITDA margin
series.note: ~53–55% recent quarter (range) — the monetization-efficiency proof
